public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as assistant curator.
appearance: Nira Veilthorn carries the marks of assistant curator in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a assistant curator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of assistant curator in and around Shirshal, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Shirshal while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Nira Veilthorn can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.
Nira Veilthorn can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Nira Veilthorn communicates in public. In Shirshal, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Magi Magistratus.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Shirshal: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague

first_impression: Nira Veilthorn presents as Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a assistant curator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Assistant Curator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Shirsh.
what_they_refuse: Nira Veilthorn refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Assistant Curator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Nira Veilthorn can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Nira Veilthorn adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Nira Veilthorn is Known locally through day-to-day work as assistant curator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Assistant Curator.
social_texture: Nira Veilthorn speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Shirshal, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Nira touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Nira Veilthorn when the party needs a socially grounded face for Shirshal: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
